frame_analyses.description: Overview of laparoscopic trainer box with equipment laid out. No task activity visible yet.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Equipment preparation phase; no scoring has begun.

frame_analyses.description: Trainee in gloves positioning at trainer box. Hands visible, gauze board visible in background.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pre-task positioning; gauze not yet grasped.

frame_analyses.description: Closeup of trainer box controls and camera focus adjustment labeled 'Adjustable focus'.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Equipment demonstration; not part of task performance.

frame_analyses.description: Trainee's hands are now positioned over the trainer. Camera view shows the laparoscopic box from above at an angle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hands are moving toward instruments; traction is being established.

frame_analyses.description: Trainee grasping laparoscopic instruments. Gauze is visible in the training chamber; alligator clips securing the corners are visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instrument grip established; trainee has control of Maryland dissector and scissors.

frame_analyses.description: Scissors positioned at the edge of the gauze. Trainee is preparing to make the initial entry cut from the gauze edge toward the marked circle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good approach angle; scissors are aligned perpendicular to the gauze surface.

frame_analyses.description: Initial cutting motion visible. Scissors cutting into gauze from the edge; Maryland dissector providing retraction/traction.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth scissor activation; dissector maintaining appropriate tension on gauze.

frame_analyses.description: Scissors actively following the pre-marked circular boundary. Cut path is visible; trainee is methodically advancing around the circle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Scissors are tracking the marked line with deliberate, controlled movements. Dissector continues to provide counter-tension.

frame_analyses.description: Continued cutting along the circular path. Approximately 60% of the circle has been cut free. Gauze remains intact in the jumbo clip.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good spatial awareness of the marked line. Cutting speed is moderate and controlled.

frame_analyses.description: Nearly complete circle cut. Only a small section remains attached to the main gauze piece. Scissors are nearly at closure point.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Approach to final closure is smooth. Minimal deviation from the marked line is evident.

frame_analyses.description: Final cut complete. The marked circle has been fully detached from the gauze and is being removed from the training chamber by the trainee.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean separation of the cut-out circle. Task completion is evident.

frame_analyses.description: Trainee's hand holding the cut-out circle disc. Gauze board remains in trainer with the circle removed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task is complete. The circle has been successfully freed from the gauze.

video_content_assessment.classification_reason: This video demonstrates equipment setup, trainer overview, and camera adjustment controls (frame 3: 'Adjustable focus' label). While a cutting task is shown in frames 4–12, the presence of explicit instructional framing (equipment tour, labeled controls, demonstration focus) and the apparent intent to show proper technique rather than a live performance scoring session indicates this is primarily educational/instructional content, not a direct performance evaluation.

cannot_score_fully: This video is classified as instructional demonstration rather than a solo trainee performance for formal scoring. While a cutting task is visible (frames 4–12), the context includes equipment tour elements that suggest this is educational content showing the task setup and execution, not an isolated performance evaluation.
completion_evidence: Frames 10–12 clearly show the marked circle fully detached from the gauze and removed from the trainer, meeting the task completion requirement.

deviation_assessment.reason: The video quality, angle, and camera positioning do not provide sufficient magnification or direct overhead view to accurately measure deviation in millimeters from the marked circle line. The cuts appear visually clean and on-line, but precise quantification is not possible from these frames.

penalties.description: Video angle and resolution do not permit accurate measurement of cut path deviation from the marked 4cm circle boundary at cardinal points.
penalties.reason: Oblique camera angle and lack of magnified direct overhead view prevent reliable mm-level deviation assessment.

task_specific_assessments.gauze_damage: None observed beyond the intended cut
task_specific_assessments.clip_integrity: Both alligator clips remained secure throughout task
task_specific_assessments.cut_quality: Visually clean; appears to follow the marked line with reasonable precision
task_specific_assessments.observable_deviations: None clearly visible, but cannot be measured precisely due to camera angle

score_components.formula_applied: N/A — instructional video; not a formal performance evaluation
score_components.rationale: This video is primarily instructional in nature (equipment demo, trainer overview). While a complete cutting task is visible, the framing and context indicate it is meant as a teaching demonstration rather than an official scored performance. Formal scoring requires a clear, isolated performance video with unambiguous start/stop times.

confidence_rationale: Confidence is low because: (1) video is instructional rather than performance-oriented; (2) camera angles are not optimized for precision measurement; (3) no explicit timestamp markers or proctor narration confirming start/end times; (4) equipment tour elements precede the actual cutting task, suggesting educational context rather than exam simulation.
cannot_determine: Precise completion time in seconds — frames show elapsed time is approximately 95–110 seconds for the cutting portion, but the instructional framing makes formal timing unreliable
Line deviation measurements at cardinal points — oblique camera angle and lack of magnified direct overhead view prevent mm-level accuracy assessment
Whether this represents the trainee's best effort or a demonstration for training purposes

demonstrated_techniques: Proper instrument grip and positioning at the laparoscopic trainer
Systematic entry cut from gauze edge toward the marked circle
Continuous traction on the gauze using Maryland dissector to maintain tension
Methodical scissors advancement along the pre-marked circular boundary
Controlled cutting speed to maintain precision and prevent over-cutting
Final release and extraction of the cut-out circle
verbal_cues: Label visible: 'Adjustable focus' (frame 3) — demonstrating camera control feature
Text overlay: 'Laparoscopic trainer box' (repeated in multiple frames) — identifying the equipment
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding deviation from the marked line by maintaining steady scissor control
Preventing gauze slippage by using dissector counter-traction throughout the cut
Preventing over-cutting beyond the intended circle boundary
teaching_content.rationale_for_skill_level: The cutting is methodical and controlled, with no visible rushing or erratic movements. The trainee successfully completes the full circle cut without damage to surrounding gauze or excessive deviation. This suggests intermediate proficiency — not expert-speed (60–90s) but competent, deliberate technique.

improvement_notes: For formal scoring: isolate the performance video without equipment tour elements
For training: emphasize maintaining consistent dissector tension to prevent gauze shifting
Video resolution/angle: future training videos should include direct overhead magnified view for precise deviation measurement
final_note: This video demonstrates successful completion of the Pattern Cut task with visually acceptable technique and cut quality. However, it is classified as instructional content rather than an isolated formal performance evaluation. For FLS exam simulation or official scoring, the video should be presented without prior equipment demonstration frames and with clearer timestamp markers.
